summary: Stainless steel electric kettle on a countertop with three visible defects: a charred/burned area at the lower metal base, a small dent on the right side of the body, and a crack in the plastic handle near the base.
scene_details: Silver stainless-steel kettle with black plastic handle placed on a light countertop. A cardboard box and packing foam are visible in the background. The burnt/melted damage is concentrated on the lower metal trim near the front, a distinct dent is visible on the right side of the kettle body, and a vertical crack is present at the lower part of the left handle where it meets the base.

defects.label: burnt/melted base
defects.evidence: Dark charred and melted residue concentrated on the lower metal trim at the front of the kettle, with blackened and bubbled material clearly visible.

defects.label: dent in body
defects.evidence: Small rounded indentation on the right stainless-steel side of the kettle, causing a visible distortion in the metal surface and reflected light.

defects.label: cracked plastic handle
defects.evidence: Vertical crack at the lower portion of the black plastic handle where it joins the kettle base; the fracture line and separation are visible.
